competency_groups.title: Chronology of Human Life and Relations with Other Civilisations
competency_groups.description: Explains and analyses the chronology of human life on the Indian subcontinent, from prehistory to its civilisational beginnings and beyond, and its relations with other civilisations over time.
competencies: C1.2 Explains and analyses the chronology of human life on the Indian subcontinent, from prehistory to its civilisational beginnings and beyond, and its relations with other civilisations over time, such as those in Mesopotamia, Greece, Central Asia, China, Southeast Asia, Arabia, and Eastern Africa
competency_groups.unit_name: India and the World-I (1900 BCE- 1200 CE)
competency_groups.chapter_name: India and the World-I (1900 BCE- 1200 CE)

competency_groups.title: Continuity and Change in Indian History
competency_groups.description: Traces aspects of continuity and change in different phases of history across the Indian subcontinent.
competencies: C1.3 Traces aspects of continuity and change in different phases of history across the Indian subcontinent (including cultural trends, social and religious trends and reforms, and economic and political transformations)
competency_groups.unit_name: Resistance and sovereignty: Resilience (1000 CE – 1700 CE)

competency_groups.title: Growth of Indigenous Ideas in India
competency_groups.description: Explains the growth of new indigenous ideas across India in Mathematics, Philosophy, Science and Technology, Medicine, Architecture, Agriculture, Literature and Art.
competencies: C1.4 Explains the growth of new indigenous ideas across India in Mathematics, Philosophy, Science and Technology, Medicine, Architecture, Agriculture, Literature and Art, and Social Science (such as zero and the Indian number system, ahimsa , the six systems of Indian philosophy, Ayurveda, yoga, the 22 shrutis of Indian music, horticulture, use of herbs and spices, etymology, meters, and grammar) and how they affected the course of the Indian history
competency_groups.unit_name: Resistance and sovereignty: Resilience (1000 CE – 1700 CE)

competency_groups.title: Continuity and Change in World History
competency_groups.description: Traces aspects of continuity and change in different phases of world history.
competencies: C-2.3 Traces aspects of continuity and change in different phases of world history (including cultural trends, social and religious reforms, and economic and political transformations)
competency_groups.unit_name: India and the World-I (1900 BCE- 1200 CE)
competency_groups.chapter_name: India and the World-I (1900 BCE- 1200 CE)

competency_groups.title: Meaning of Nation in Indian Context
competency_groups.description: Analyses the meaning of nation and how the concept evolved over time across the world and in the specific context of India.
competencies: C 3.1 Analyses the meaning of nation and how the concept evolved over time across the world and in the specific context of India, including its roots in the rich civilisational history of the Indian subcontinent
competency_groups.unit_name: Resistance and sovereignty: Resilience (1000 CE – 1700 CE)

competency_groups.title: Geographical Concepts and Landforms
competency_groups.description: Explains important geographical concepts, characteristics of key landforms, their origin, and other physical factors.
competencies: C-4.2 Explains important geographical concepts, characteristics of key landforms, their origin, and other physical factors of a region

competency_groups.title: Inter-linkages in Physical Environment
competency_groups.description: Draws inter- linkages between various components of the physical environment.
competencies: C-4.3 Draws inter- linkages between various components of the physical environment, such as climate and relief, climate and vegetation, vegetation, and wildlife.

competency_groups.title: Impact of Human Interventions on Environment
competency_groups.description: Critically evaluates the impact of human interventions on the environment, including climate change, pollution, shortages of natural resources.
competencies: C-4.5 Critically evaluates the impact of human interventions on the environment, including climate change, pollution, shortages of natural resources (particularly water), and loss of biodiversity; identifies practices that have led to these environmental crises and the measures that must be taken to reverse them.

competency_groups.title: Indian Constitution and Early Democracy Experiments
competency_groups.description: Understands that the Indian Constitution draws from cultural heritage and recalls India's early experiments with democracy.
competencies: C-5.1 Understands that the Indian Constitution draws from the great cultural heritage and common aspirations of the Indian nation, and recalls India’s early experiments with democracy (assemblies in Mahajanapadas , kingdoms and empires at several levels of the society, guilds sanghas and ganas , village councils and committees, Uthiramerur inscriptions)

competency_groups.title: Fundamental Constitutional Values
competency_groups.description: Appreciates fundamental Constitutional values and identify their significance for the prosperity of the Indian nation.
competencies: C-5.2 Appreciates fundamental Constitutional values and identify their significance for the prosperity of the Indian nation.

competency_groups.title: Fundamental Rights and Duties
competency_groups.description: Explains that fundamental rights are the most basic human rights, and they flourish when people also perform their fundamental duties.
competencies: C-5.3 Explains that fundamental rights are the most basic human rights, and they flourish when people also perform their fundamental duties

competency_groups.title: Unity in Diversity and Cultural Integration
competency_groups.description: Understands how the Indian ethos respected diversity, promoted unity in diversity, and peaceful coexistence.
competencies: C-6.1 Understands how the Indian ethos and the cultural integration across India did not attempt uniformity, but respected and promoted a rich diversity in Indian society, and how this harmonisation and unity in diversity, with a historical respect for all cultures, women have counted among India’s great strengths by promoting peaceful coexistence
competency_groups.unit_name: India and the World-I (1900 BCE- 1200 CE)
competency_groups.chapter_name: India and the World-I (1900 BCE- 1200 CE)

competency_groups.title: Organised vs Unorganised Sectors
competency_groups.description: Distinguishes between 'unorganised' and 'organised' sectors of the economy and their role in production.
competencies: C-7.3 Distinguishes between ‘unorganised’ and ‘organised’ sectors of the economy and their role in production for the local market in small, medium, and large-scale production centres (industries), and recognises the special importance of the so-called ‘unorganised’ sector in Indian economy and its connections with the self-organising features of Indian society

competency_groups.title: Personal Financial Management and Economic Concepts
competency_groups.description: Evaluates economic development impact on lives, understands financial management concepts including interest rates, budgeting, risk-return trade-offs.
competencies: C-8.1 Gathers, comprehends and analyses data related to income, capital, poverty, and employment in one’s locality, region and at the national level.
C- 8.2 Understands and analyses the concepts and practices of the range of economic systems — from free market to entirely state-controlled markets.
C-8.3 Understands these features in the context of ancient India...
C-8.4 Describes India’s recent path towards again becoming one of the three largest economies...
competency_groups.unit_name: Smart Ways to Manage Your Finances (6 Hours)
competency_groups.chapter_name: Smart Ways to Manage Your Finances (6 Hours)

competency_groups.title: India's Contributions to Social Science
competency_groups.description: Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters studied within the curriculum.
competencies: C – 9.1 Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters (concepts, explanations, methods) studied within the curriculum, in an integrated manner
competency_groups.unit_name: India and the World-I (1900 BCE- 1200 CE)
competency_groups.chapter_name: India and the World-I (1900 BCE- 1200 CE)
